patch.value: Key findings: (1) Governance preset switching works. (2) Type options are schema-enforced. (3) JSON error envelopes consistently include type/code/title/detail/required/why/examples/next_steps. (4) pm contracts --json provides v4 schema with full flag metadata. (5) pm completion generates bash/zsh scripts. (6) Extension scaffold produces working projects. (7) Agent-friendly: all output is deterministic TOON/JSON, all commands have --json, errors have examples. (8) Non-software adaptability: custom types via settings work, but type_options require extension registration for runtime schema.
Feature: Extensibility architecture verified - governance, custom types, agent UX

Extensibility assessment: governance presets (minimal/default/strict/custom) switch correctly. Custom type options require pre-defined schema (enforced). Status aliases normalize correctly. Error envelopes in JSON include actionable guidance with examples. pm contracts provides complete machine-readable API surface. Definition of done criteria are configurable. Extension lifecycle is complete (install/manage/doctor/init/explore/activate/uninstall).
Governance presets, error envelopes, and extension lifecycle are fully verified

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Evidence: strict governance mode adds required fields. Error envelopes with --json include URN-typed errors. pm contracts --schema-only --action create --json provides full parameter schema. pm help --json provides structured help v1 format. All commands accept --json.

patch.value: Extensibility verified: governance presets work, type schema enforcement works, JSON error envelopes have actionable guidance, pm contracts provides v4 API schema, extension lifecycle complete
